skill.name: flowio
skill.description: Parse Flow Cytometry Standard files with FlowIO. Read FCS metadata, extract event arrays, convert data to tables, and create or modify FCS files.
skill.summary: Use FlowIO to inspect, transform, and export flow cytometry FCS data.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command, script, and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are markdown examples or FlowIO terminology rather than executable skill code. No prompt injection, network exfiltration, credential access, or hidden scripts were found. The remaining risk is low because the community skill recommends package installation and local FCS file read/write workflows.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Installation Examples
low_findings.description: The static external command findings occur in markdown code fences and examples, including pip or uv pip installation commands. They do not execute automatically and do not include shell interpolation or remote payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced markdown examples, not runnable skill code. The commands install the public FlowIO package and contain no user-controlled arguments.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Terminology Matches
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings appear to match ordinary FlowIO terms, FCS metadata, or names such as descriptive channel labels. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged context documents FCS channel names, metadata, and FlowIO reference sections. I found no code path that performs cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: Script Finding Is A Static Match On Import Syntax
low_findings.description: The dynamic import finding points to a normal Python import statement inside a markdown example. It imports FlowIO classes for exception handling and is not a JavaScript dynamic import expression.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is a Python code block using from flowio import syntax. It is documentation text and cannot dynamically load arbitrary modules.

low_findings.title: Local File Processing Requires User Care
low_findings.description: The skill guides users to open, inspect, modify, and export local FCS and CSV files. This is expected for the data workflow, but users should avoid untrusted files and choose output paths carefully.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly instructs local file reads and writes, which is a legitimate capability. The risk is limited because there is no network transfer or hidden automation.

content.value_statement: Flow cytometry teams often need to inspect FCS files before analysis. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through FlowIO workflows for metadata, arrays, CSV export, and FCS creation.
actual_capabilities: Reads FlowIO usage guidance for FCS versions 2.0, 3.0, and 3.1.
Explains how to extract event data as NumPy arrays.
Shows how to inspect TEXT metadata and channel labels.
Guides conversion from FCS event data to pandas DataFrames and CSV files.
Documents creation of new FCS files from event arrays and channel names.
Covers multi-dataset FCS handling and common FlowIO exceptions.
limitations: It does not perform compensation, gating, or advanced cytometry analysis.
It depends on the external Python FlowIO package being available.
It provides guidance and examples, not a bundled parser implementation.
Large FCS files may require memory planning outside the skill.

use_cases.title: Inspect FCS uploads
use_cases.description: Review file version, event count, channel labels, and key metadata before accepting uploaded cytometry data.

use_cases.title: Prepare analysis tables
use_cases.description: Convert event arrays and channel labels into tabular data for notebooks, dashboards, or quality checks.

use_cases.title: Create processed FCS outputs
use_cases.description: Save filtered or transformed event data as a new FCS file while preserving useful metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Read one FCS file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the FlowIO skill to show me how to read sample.fcs, print its version, event count, channel names, and event array shape.

prompt_templates.title: Export events to CSV
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert cytometry data for table analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use FlowIO guidance to convert an FCS file into a pandas DataFrame with channel labels, then export the events to CSV.

prompt_templates.title: Handle difficult FCS files
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me diagnose a FlowIO parsing failure. Include offset discrepancy options, multi-dataset handling, and when to read metadata only.

prompt_templates.title: Build a processing pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a FlowIO-based workflow that scans a folder of FCS files, extracts metadata summaries, filters events, and writes processed outputs.

output_examples.input: Read a sample FCS file and summarize its contents.
output_examples.output: A concise report listing the FCS version, total events, channel count, channel labels, instrument metadata, and event array dimensions.

output_examples.input: Convert a cytometry file to a table for downstream analysis.
output_examples.output: Step-by-step guidance that creates a labeled DataFrame, preserves selected metadata, and exports a CSV file.

output_examples.input: Fix parsing issues with a multi-dataset FCS file.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting plan covering metadata-only reads, offset handling options, and reading each dataset separately.

best_practices: Use metadata-only reads when event data is not needed.
Preserve original TEXT metadata when creating modified FCS files.
Validate channel counts and labels before filtering or exporting events.
anti_patterns: Do not use FlowIO alone for compensation, gating, or FlowJo workspace analysis.
Do not ignore parsing errors without recording why relaxed parsing was needed.
Do not overwrite original FCS files when testing transformations.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants use FlowIO to read, inspect, convert, and create Flow Cytometry Standard files.

faq.question: Does it include the FlowIO package?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for using the external Python FlowIO package.

faq.question: Can it perform gating or compensation?
faq.answer: No. The skill recommends FlowKit or other specialized tools for advanced cytometry analysis.

faq.question: Which FCS versions are covered?
faq.answer: The documentation covers FlowIO support for FCS versions 2.0, 3.0, and 3.1.

faq.question: Can it export CSV files?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to convert event arrays into pandas DataFrames and export CSV files.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found no malicious behavior, but users should handle local files and package installation carefully.
